description:
Find where a C# script class or one of its members is used inside Unity asset files (scenes, prefabs, ScriptableObject assets) — the serialized references that code_find_references cannot see. Use this before renaming or deleting a public method, UnityEvent handler or serialized field: zero code references does NOT mean unused, scene bindings break silently.

Modes:
- Without 'member': lists every attach point of the script — which GameObjects in which scenes/prefabs carry the component, plus ScriptableObject instances of the class.
- With 'member': reports serialized uses of that member. UnityEvent bindings (m_MethodName, e.g. Button.onClick) and AnimationEvent function names (.anim clips) are resolved from the indexed asset database — this covers broken/unbound UnityEvent bindings named only by type string (m_Target: {fileID: 0}, which carry no GUID so the reference graph never sees them) and animation events project-wide, both of which a rename or delete would break silently. Serialized field values (member: value) are matched by scanning the assets that reference the script.

Notes:
- Requires the pre-built asset reference database (rescan from the UI if results look stale).
- UnityEvent matches are annotated with their resolved target (this script, optionally the host GameObject); broken/unbound bindings are reported separately and annotated as such.
- The index matches the exact class short-name, not subclasses: a binding that targets a subclass inside an asset which never references this script directly is not found; check subclasses separately if relevant.

file_path.description: Workspace-relative or absolute path to the .cs script file.

member.description: Optional method or serialized-field name declared in that script (e.g. 'TakeDamage', 'maxHealth'). Omit to list the script's attach points instead.

max_results.description: Maximum usages to return. Default 100, max 400.
